titleStmt.title: United States Delegation Memorandum Authorship not indicated.

div.p: Pending Problems Before the Conference, Close of Business, July 30, 1945

p.hi: i. problems before the big three

div.p: The U. S. has presented proposals on all three of these questions and has insisted that they be considered as a unit.See documents Nos. 731, 961, 962, and 1152, post. The British also presented yesterday a new proposal on German Reparations. Document No. 960, post. There seemed to be general agreement on the U. S. proposal concerning reparations, made applicable to the entire British and American Zones rather than to the Ruhr alone, with a corresponding reduction in the percentages. The specific question referred to the Big Three was whether or not a definite figure, in dollars or tons, for the Soviet share of reparations from western Germany should be specified.
4. Economic Principles for Germany
It was agreed that decision on this question will depend on the decision on German reparations.
5. Italian Reparations
Mr. Molotov’s motion on this question was referred back once more to the Big Three.

p.hi: ii. problems before the foreign ministers

div.p: 1. Additional Points for Inclusion in the German Political Principles

The British proposed two additional points Document No. 879, post. the first of which was accepted and the second referred to the Control Commission for Germany. The Soviets also presented a new point Document No. 886, post. which is still before the Foreign Ministers.
2. Southeast Europe and Trieste—Istria District
British and Soviet papers on these subjects Documents Nos. 1083 and 1211, post, respectively. are still before the foreign ministers.
3. War Crimes

The Soviet and British papersSee documents Nos. 1016, 1017, and 1018, post. on this subject were presented. It was agreed in principle that a statement on the subject should be issued by the Conference. The question of the exact form of this statement is still before the Foreign Ministers.
4. Fascist Activities in the U. S., British and French Zones of Germany and Austria
The U. S. and British Secretaries of State have agreed to reply as soon as possible to a Soviet note Documents Nos. 790 and 1055, post. on this subject.
5. German Troops in Norway
The U. S. and British Secretaries of State have agreed to reply as soon as possible to a Soviet note Document No. 1056, post. on this subject.
6. Disposition of Soviet Citizens from the Baltic, the Ukraine and White Russia now in Allied Territories
The U. S. and British Secretaries of State have agreed to reply as soon as possible to a Soviet note Document No. 1167, post. on this subject.
7. Ruhr Industrial Area
The Soviet paper Document No. 1027, post. on this question is before the Foreign Ministers but the British have stated they could not discuss the subject without the French.
8. Allied Oil Equipment in Rumania
The Foreign Ministers have not yet acted on the latest British proposal Document No. 841, post. for dealing with this subject.
1. German Fleet and Merchant Marine
British and Soviet papers Documents Nos. 1014 and 1013, post respectively. on this subject are before the subcommittee.
2. German External Assets
A U. S. proposal Document No. 1000, post. on this subject was referred to the Economic Sub-committee.
3. Oil for Western Europe
The Economic Sub-committee has not yet reported on the U. S. proposal Document No. 1320, post. on this subject.
4. Implementation of the Yalta Declaration on Liberated Europe and Satellite States
The Sub-committee on this subject has not yet reported.
5. Unrestricted Navigation of International Inland Waterways
The Sub-committee on this subject has not yet reported.
6. Transfer of German Populations from Czechoslovakia, Poland and Hungary
The Sub-committee on this subject has not yet reported.
7. Directive to the Military Commanders in Germany
The Sub-committee on this subject is awaiting completion of the work of the Conference in relation to Germany.
8. Protocol and Communiqué of the Conference
The Sub-committees are engaged in preparation of these documents.
